title: The interaction of demand and supply
paperName: Cambridge International AS Level Economics (P1 & P2)
summary: 9708 AS — simultaneous demand and supply shifts, market disequilibrium, and price mechanism with interactive GeoGebra model.
learningObjectives: Analyse simultaneous shifts in demand and supply.
Explain excess supply and excess demand.
Describe the price mechanism in allocating resources.
Evaluate government intervention effects on markets.

simpleExplanation.title: Markets adjust
simpleExplanation.summary: When price is above equilibrium, surplus pushes price down; below equilibrium, shortage pushes price up. Shifts in both curves require careful step-by-step analysis.
steps: Price above equilibrium → surplus → downward pressure on P.
Price below equilibrium → shortage → upward pressure on P.
Both curves shift → one variable (P or Q) may be ambiguous — state 'depends on magnitude'.
Use the interactive model to test different shift combinations.

flashcards.front: What happens when price is above equilibrium?
flashcards.back: Excess supply (surplus) — Qs > Qd — puts downward pressure on price until equilibrium is restored.

flashcards.front: What happens when price is below equilibrium?
flashcards.back: Excess demand (shortage) — Qd > Qs — puts upward pressure on price.

flashcards.front: If demand and supply both increase, what happens to Q?
flashcards.back: Quantity definitely rises — equilibrium Q is unambiguously higher.

flashcards.front: If demand and supply both increase, what happens to P?
flashcards.back: Ambiguous — depends on relative magnitudes of shifts; state 'depends on magnitude' in exams.
Rationing, signalling, and incentivising — prices adjust to eliminate surpluses and shortages.

flashcards.front: How do you analyse simultaneous curve shifts?
flashcards.back: Shift one curve at a time on a diagram; compare original and new equilibrium; note ambiguous outcomes.

sections.content: Topic **2.4** builds on 2.1–2.3 into full **market analysis** — the standard 9708 8–12 mark micro essay often asks you to **shift curves** and discuss **efficiency**.
Structure essays: **define** equilibrium → **diagram** original → **shift** with reason → **new equilibrium** → **evaluate** (winners/losers, time period).

sections.question:
In the market for coffee, a health report increases demand while good weather simultaneously increases supply.

Analyse the effect on equilibrium price and quantity.
sections.solution:
**Step 1 — Demand shift:** Health report → demand shifts **right** → P↑, Q↑.

**Step 2 — Supply shift:** Good harvest → supply shifts **right** → P↓, Q↑.

**Step 3 — Combined effect on Q:** Both shifts raise quantity → **Q definitely increases**.

**Step 4 — Combined effect on P:** Demand raises P; supply lowers P → **P is ambiguous** — depends on relative shift magnitudes.

If demand shift is larger → net P rises. If supply shift is larger → net P falls.

**Diagram:** Draw D₁→D₂ (right) and S₁→S₂ (right). Mark E₁ and E₂. Label Q₂ > Q₁ clearly.

steps.caption: Increase in demand → higher equilibrium P and Q.
steps.embedHint: Shift demand right — trace new intersection.

steps.caption: Increase in supply → lower equilibrium P, higher Q.
steps.embedHint: Shift supply right — price falls, quantity rises.

steps.caption: Simultaneous shifts need careful analysis — direction depends on magnitudes.
steps.embedHint: Shift both curves — predict ambiguous cases for exams.

steps.caption: Excess supply (surplus) or excess demand (shortage) off equilibrium.
steps.embedHint: Move price above equilibrium — surplus appears.
